title: Cell viability assessment
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"1qnj7","text":"This is the protocol for assessing cell viability using a MTS cell proliferation assay kit.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: 1. Cells are seeded and differentiated for 4 days; assess cell viability using an MTS cell proliferation assay kit (ab197010, Abcam).
2. Treat the differentiated cells with synthetic DOPA pheomelanin or synthetic DOPA eumelanin or vehicle PBS to determine the time and dose responses of the cells to the treatment.
3. Remove culture medium and add a 110 µL solution containing 10 µL MTS and 100 µL medium to each well, incubate in humidified air containing 5% CO2 at 37°C for 1 hour.
4. Measure absorbance at 490 nm using a microplate reader.
creator: Jackson Schumacher
